stageChain.summary: Planted low-risk and high-risk engine defects surface through the existing scenario verdicts and four gates.
Journal evidence explains the failing gate and preserves the real-title dogfood context.
Ledger events trace the autonomous loop stages without human input.
Loop-coverage attribution ties the defect to the responsible milestone evidence.
Diagnosis emits source-apply patch previews rather than trusted writes.
The proposal reruns openchrome, four gates, design-integrity, rollback, kill-switch, and trust-gradient checks.
The low-risk reversible proposal improves attributed evidence and routes to existing source-apply/trust-gradient auto-apply.
The high-risk/source-affecting proposal is verified and reversible, then queued for one-click human go/no-go provenance.

boundary: End-to-end Era L M71 self-improvement demo for the existing Era I engine-builder deckbuilder: detect, explain, trace, attribute, propose, re-verify, apply, and queue reuse openchrome, scenario verdicts, four gates plus design-integrity, journal.md, ledger.jsonl, loop-coverage attribution, evolve, source-apply, trust-gradient, rollback, and kill-switch artifacts. The low-risk reversible defect is auto-applied only after before/after evidence improves. The high-risk/source-affecting defect is verified, reversible, and queued for thin one-click human go/no-go provenance without autonomous source mutation. No new verification engine, no new data plane, no new store, no blocking human dependency on unrelated autonomous loop work; fun/taste and release go/no-go remain human Ring 2; #1 and #23 remain open.
